trans-unit.target: TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_000<tab><var 29 EB02 /var>... I didn't know you took part in cosmic exploration! Well, as a member of the scouting party, I reckon I can fill you in on a few things.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_Q1_000_000<tab>What will you ask about?
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_001<tab>Tell me about yourself.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_002<tab>Auxesia.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_003<tab>Timberpond.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_004<tab>The Emerald Nervure.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_005<tab>Full Bloom Gardens.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_006<tab>The Netroot Radius.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_007<tab>Sylvan Stacks.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_008<tab>The Stratostone Stand.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_009<tab>The Core Shroud.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_A1_000_010<tab>Nothing.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_001<tab>I traveled the realm for decades as an arborist of sorts, visiting my forest village on occasion, until I settled down at Meryall Agronomics in Labyrinthos.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_002<tab>To think contributing botanical knowledge to the ark project would lead me to the stars... My sweetheart back home would be shocked if she knew!

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_003<tab>But while Auxesian soil is ideal for putting our studies to practice, plants don't grow overnight─so when I have time, I show folks around and check on the trees.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_005<tab>Well, I hear it's the ninth astral body in the EG-1 star system and over seven carrots from Etheirys, which I've been assured is quite far.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_006<tab>We got here following a navigational chart left on Oizys by the Voyagers─but whatever we expected to find, it wasn't an entire abandoned city!

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_007<tab>At any rate, Auxesia is easily the most habitable of the three stars we've reached. The environs are so stable, the rainbow crystal need hardly operate at all.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_008<tab>Yet despite the abundance of aether, not a single soul remains─man or beast─leaving only the plants to benefit. It's almost as though everyone vanished without a trace...

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_010<tab>Timberpond refers to the eastern district encompassing our outpost. Being comparatively clear of trees, like a logging ground, makes for an ideal starship harbor.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_011<tab>The city stands upon the canopies of colossal trees, aetherically augmented to an altitude of several hundred fulms above ground, with a roadway linking each substructure.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_012<tab>Do you reckon the natives settled up here in pursuit of sunlight? Honestly, I can't believe nearly all of these buildings are made of wood!

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_013<tab>The Emerald Nervure is a long road that runs through the heart of the city. The way it connects the substructures, like the veins of a leaf, paints a vivid picture of how the Voyagers may have raced along them in their vehicles.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_014<tab>In fact, I get the sense they had a rather sizable population, too. How many cities of comparable scale can be found on Etheirys, I wonder?
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_015<tab>Given its age, though, I feared the Nervure might crumble beneath our feet. Luckily, I'm told it has all the resilience of reinforced fibers!
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_016<tab>The northwestern cluster looks a bit different from the rest. Like a pastoral landscape, it features more plant life than overtly man-made structures.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_017<tab>For such an advanced civilization, what purpose might these swaths of empty land have served? Farmland, parkland, or perhaps holy ground?

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_018<tab>Our research is ongoing, but the flora may well hold the answers. Indeed, with a better grasp of the city layout, I daresay full many theories will emerge...

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_019<tab>The Netroot Radius is the southeast substructure, home to the Sprouting Towerling, its supporting facilities, and potentially an aetheric confluence, if the overgrowth is any indication.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_020<tab>In fact, theory has it that the tower is part of a system which redirects that surfeit of aether to the city core.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_021<tab>Likewise, the logistics of powering an energization station dictated we build our cosmoport here─and for all we know, perhaps we weren't the first to do so.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_022<tab>Sylvan Stacks in the northwest features not only the Sunning Towerling but rows of crystals amid the trees, offering an uncommon contrast of green and blue.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_023<tab>The largest is said to store vast quantities of data, not entirely unlike the “concepts” used by the Loporrits. A crystalline library, one might say!

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_024<tab>With the Sunning Towerling revived, I expect the surrounding crystals are next to be analyzed. I can but imagine how much information lies dormant within...

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_025<tab>The Stratostone Stand refers to the southwestern dome. Like Sylvan Stacks, the tower there stands surrounded by crystals that offer both power and storage.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_026<tab>We suspect they were all brought here from Oizys. Yet while the city lay abandoned, they've continued to grow in size by absorbing aether.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_027<tab>The Rooting Towerling, meanwhile, is notable for its separation of the transmitter from the main interior. Both are doubtless vital components, sheltered as they are from the elements.
TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_028<tab>Towering to the north, you'll find the Core Shroud, the sacred white forest at the heart of Auxesia.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_029<tab>The upper level is a series of facilities encased in resin domes. We're currently investigating Grandma Laurel at the center, but the rest are no less functional.

TEXT_CTSWKS75TIMBERLODGE_00983_SPINA_000_030<tab>I'm told the names of this and the other towers were designed to reflect a family─and a remarkable one at that, given all its technological advancements!
